positions.claim: Memory manufacturers (Samsung, SK Hynix) possess sustainable pricing power due to structural supply constraints that will persist through multiple product cycles
implicitAssumptions: Memory production capacity expansion timelines (18-36 months for new fabs) are fundamentally longer than demand growth cycles, creating persistent supply-demand imbalances
The oligopolistic structure of the memory market (Big 3 control) is stable against disruption from Chinese competitors or new entrants over a 3-5 year horizon
Enterprise and AI customers have inelastic demand curves for memory products, meaning they will absorb price increases rather than delay purchases or seek architectural alternatives

positions.claim: Samsung Foundry's return to profitability represents a major inflection point that is significantly underpriced by the market
implicitAssumptions: Samsung's foundry technology roadmap (gate-all-around, backside power delivery) will achieve competitive parity with TSMC within 2-3 nodes, making profitability sustainable rather than temporary
The market systematically underweights turnaround stories in semiconductor manufacturing due to recency bias from Samsung's previous foundry struggles
Foundry profitability will materially impact Samsung's consolidated valuation despite being a smaller segment than memory

positions.claim: AI-driven memory demand represents a structural shift that extends beyond HBM to all memory categories (DRAM, NAND), not a temporary spike
implicitAssumptions: AI inference deployment will scale at rates comparable to or exceeding training infrastructure buildout, driving sustained memory demand growth
Memory intensity per compute unit in AI workloads will continue increasing faster than Moore's Law density improvements can offset
The AI infrastructure buildout is in early innings (sub-20% penetration) with multi-year runway remaining before saturation

positions.claim: Advanced packaging technology (FO-PLP, SiP with hybrid bonding) represents the next major competitive battleground in semiconductors, with HBM integration as the key application
implicitAssumptions: Packaging technology development cycles are shorter than leading-edge logic node development, allowing faster competitive repositioning for companies investing in this capability
System-level integration through advanced packaging provides greater performance gains per dollar invested than continued logic node scaling below 3nm
Control of packaging technology creates defensible moats because it requires co-optimization with both logic and memory design, raising barriers to entry

positions.claim: Holding Korean semiconductor stocks (Samsung, SK Hynix) while avoiding Nvidia represents a contrarian but rational positioning strategy
implicitAssumptions: Nvidia's valuation already fully reflects AI infrastructure buildout expectations, while memory suppliers remain undervalued relative to their exposure to the same trend
Memory manufacturers capture a more defensible share of AI infrastructure spending due to oligopolistic market structure compared to Nvidia's exposure to potential competition from custom ASICs
Korean semiconductor stocks offer better risk-adjusted returns despite lower absolute growth rates because market expectations are lower and margin expansion potential is underappreciated

positions.claim: Supply chain bottlenecks in AI infrastructure (PCB materials, drill bits, optical fiber) signal genuine demand strength rather than temporary constraints
implicitAssumptions: Component suppliers operate with minimal safety stock and just-in-time inventory models, meaning bottlenecks emerge only when demand genuinely exceeds long-term capacity planning
These bottlenecks will resolve through capacity expansion rather than demand destruction, validating the durability of AI infrastructure spending
Bottlenecks in lower-tier supply chain components (PCB materials vs. chips) are leading indicators of sustained end-market demand because they respond more slowly to demand signals

positions.claim: Markets exhibit excessive fragility due to leverage buildup, causing overreactions to macro headlines that create tactical opportunities
implicitAssumptions: Fundamental value of semiconductor companies is largely decoupled from short-term macro volatility, making macro-driven selloffs temporary mispricings rather than justified revaluations
Margin debt levels and leverage metrics are reliable leading indicators of market fragility that can be monitored to anticipate volatility spikes
Retail and algorithmic traders dominate short-term price action, while institutional fundamentals-based buyers provide eventual price support, creating predictable mean reversion patterns

positions.claim: Samsung's next-generation DRAM technology (1c node with Stand It Vertical, Stack and Bond) positions them competitively for HBM4 despite current HBM3E challenges
implicitAssumptions: Process technology leadership at the base DRAM level translates directly into HBM competitiveness, making node transitions more important than current generation yield issues
Samsung's HBM3E qualification delays are execution issues rather than fundamental technology gaps, and will not persist into the HBM4 generation
Nvidia and other AI accelerator customers will dual-source or multi-source HBM to avoid supply concentration risk, ensuring Samsung can capture share even without being first to market

positions.claim: Broadcom's SiP technology development with hybrid bonding makes them a major emerging HBM customer, representing demand diversification beyond GPU manufacturers
implicitAssumptions: Custom AI ASIC adoption will accelerate significantly as hyperscalers seek to reduce dependence on Nvidia, driving HBM demand from non-GPU sources
Broadcom's customer base growth (hyperscalers deploying custom silicon) will scale faster than their IP development costs, creating operating leverage that sustains their HBM purchasing power
SiP integration technology creates sufficient differentiation that Broadcom's AI ASIC customers will accept the solution despite premium pricing, validating the business model

positions.claim: Position sizing discipline matters more than entry timing—buying too small initially and chasing higher leads to suboptimal cost basis
implicitAssumptions: Conviction in a thesis should translate into position sizing at initial entry rather than being built gradually through price confirmation
The psychological difficulty of adding to positions at higher prices creates a systematic bias toward undersized positions in winning trades
Opportunity cost of undersized positions in high-conviction ideas exceeds the risk reduction benefit of gradual position building

positions.claim: US export restrictions on AI chips to China create artificial supply-demand imbalances that distort market signals without fundamentally constraining Chinese AI development
implicitAssumptions: Chinese buyers will find workarounds (domestic alternatives, smuggling, cloud access) that satisfy most of their AI compute needs despite export caps
The gap between policy limits and actual Chinese demand indicates the restrictions are politically motivated rather than calibrated to technical security concerns
Export restrictions accelerate rather than delay Chinese semiconductor self-sufficiency by forcing investment in domestic alternatives

positions.claim: When industry research contradicts executive claims, the research signal is more reliable than management commentary
implicitAssumptions: Company executives face career and stock-based compensation incentives to present optimistic narratives even when internal data suggests problems
Independent industry research firms have reputational incentives to be accurate that outweigh any bias toward negativity or sensationalism
The lag between when problems emerge internally and when management acknowledges them publicly is predictable and can be exploited for positioning
